subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Supported player counts** — Core game is **3-5 players**.
- **3-player deck removal** — Remove all cards with **4+ icon**: 36 Bread, 4 Pepper, 5 Mead, 3 Silk.
- **4-5 player deck** — Keep full non-removed deck for normal setup.
- **Sheriff rotations to end game** — **3 players: 3 Sheriff turns each**; **4-5 players: 2 Sheriff turns each**.
- **Royal Goods in 3-player game** — When using Royal Goods module, remove cards marked **4+**.

#### Goods Cards (216 total)
- **Legal Goods (144)** — 48 Apples, 36 Cheese, 36 Bread, 24 Chicken.
- **Contraband (60)** — 22 Pepper, 21 Mead, 12 Silk, 5 Crossbow.
- **Royal Goods (12)** — 2 each of Royal Cheddar, Gouda Cheese, Rye Bread, Pumpernickel Bread, Royal Rooster, Golden Apple.

#### Coins and Player Tools
- **Gold coins (110)** — 39x1, 42x5, 17x20, 12x50.
- **Sheriff marker** — 1 marker.
- **Merchant Stands** — 5 stands.
- **Merchant Bags** — 5 bags.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Setup
- **Player kit** — Each player takes 1 Merchant Stand and matching Merchant Bag.
- **Banker role** — Choose one banker; banker gives each player 50 Gold.
- **Bank separation** — Banker must keep personal coins separate from the bank.
- **Hand size** — Deal 6 cards to each player.
- **Draw pile** — Put remaining cards facedown as the draw pile.
- **Discard piles** — Reveal 5 cards to make discard pile 1 and 5 cards to make discard pile 2.
- **Open information** — All players may inspect discard piles at any time.

#### Player-Count Variant
--- 3 Players ---
- **Deck pruning required** — Before shuffling, remove all cards with the **4+** icon: 36 Bread, 4 Pepper, 5 Mead, 3 Silk.
- **Royal Goods module** — If used, also remove Royal Goods cards marked **4+**.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Sheriff Selection
- **Default selector** — Player with highest value of actual cash on their person starts as Sheriff.
- **Tie/no-cash fallback** — If tied or nobody has cash, choose Sheriff randomly.
- **Marker assignment** — Give Sheriff marker to the starting Sheriff.

#### Round 1 Start
1. **Begin phase flow** — Start with Phase 1 (Market).
2. **Sheriff scope** — Sheriff does not participate in Market, Load Bag, or Declaration in that round.

#### Sheriff Rotation Target
- **3-player game** — End after each player has been Sheriff **3 times**.
- **4-5 player game** — End after each player has been Sheriff **2 times**.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Sequence (Always in Order)
1. **Market** — Each merchant may discard up to 5 cards, then draw back to 6.
2. **Load Merchant Bag** — Each merchant places 1-5 cards in bag and snaps it closed.
3. **Declaration** — Each merchant declares exact card count and exactly one legal good type.
4. **Inspection** — Sheriff decides which bags to inspect, with negotiation before each decision.
5. **End of Round** — Pass Sheriff marker left, refill hands to 6, begin next round.

#### Market Phase Precision
- **Turn order** — Start with player left of Sheriff, then clockwise.
- **Draw sources** — Draw from top of either discard pile and/or draw pile, one card at a time.
- **Order restriction** — If taking from discards, you must do that before drawing from draw pile.
- **Discard return** — Place all set-aside cards face up onto one discard pile in any order.

#### End Condition Hook
- **Round continuation** — Continue rounds until endgame Sheriff-count trigger is reached.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Merchant Actions
- **Market cycling** — Discard up to 5 cards and redraw to exactly 6 cards.
- **Bag loading** — Put 1-5 goods cards in bag; 0 and 6+ are illegal.
- **Declaration limit** — Declare only legal goods, exactly one type, and exact bag count.
- **Bluff freedom** — You may lie about type/contents, but never about number of cards.

#### Negotiation and Bribes
- **Bribe forms** — Gold, legal goods from stand, contraband from stand, goods in bag, or same-round favors.
- **Hand-card restriction** — Cards in hand cannot be offered as bribe.
- **Table talk scope** — Any players may negotiate, but Sheriff decides each bag outcome.

#### Sheriff Choices Per Bag
- **Accept deal** — Return bag unopened and forfeit inspection of that bag.
- **Inspect bag** — Reject deal and open bag.

#### If Bag Passes Unchecked
- **Public reveal** — Merchant reveals bag to all players.
- **Legal placement** — Legal goods go face up in Merchant Stand.
- **Contraband placement** — Contraband goes face down at top of Merchant Stand.
- **Bribe payment** — Merchant pays promised bribe if payable from actual available assets.

#### If Sheriff Inspects and Merchant Was Truthful
- **Truth test** — Bag exactly matches declared legal type and count.
- **Penalty payment direction** — Sheriff pays merchant the penalty value on each legal card in bag.
- **Goods placement** — Merchant adds those legal goods to Merchant Stand.

#### If Sheriff Inspects and Merchant Lied
- **Declared legal keepers** — Goods truthfully matching declaration are allowed into stand.
- **Confiscation** — All undeclared/mismatched cards (legal or contraband) are discarded by Sheriff in chosen order.
- **Penalty payment direction** — Merchant pays Sheriff penalties on all confiscated cards.

#### Debt/Shortage Handling
- **Coin shortage** — Pay with legal goods from stand with total value at least debt (no change).
- **Further shortage** — Reveal/pay contraband if legal goods are insufficient.
- **Exhaustion rule** — If stand is exhausted, any remaining debt is treated as paid.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Honor Among Thieves (Deal Binding)
- **Binding scope** — Deals are binding only if they can be completed in the current round.
- **Future promises** — Promises about later rounds are not binding.
- **Acceptance moment** — A deal is formed when offer is accepted; using the word "deal" is recommended.

#### Goods-in-Bribe Reality Check
- **Actual goods rule** — Merchant only pays promised goods that actually exist in bag/stand at resolution.
- **Proof requirement** — If promised goods are missing, merchant must prove absence to Sheriff.

#### Information State
- **Discard transparency** — Discard piles are open information at all times.
- **Draw visibility** — Cards taken from discards must be visible while drawn.
- **Contraband secrecy** — Smuggled contraband stays face down in stand until final scoring reveal.

#### Optional Variants in Rulebook
- **Inspection timer** — Add time limit to Inspection phase.
- **Remove cards pregame** — Randomly remove 10 cards (optional anti-counting rule).
- **Seven-card hand** — Increase hand size from 6 to 7 (optional).
- **Promo cards** — Cards with blue promo banner may be added without removing others.

#### Royal Goods Module
- **During play** — Royal Goods count as contraband for declaration, inspection, confiscation, and penalties.
- **At scoring** — Royal Goods count as 2 or 3 units of matching legal good for King/Queen bonus races.

#### Player-Count Variant (Royal Goods Module)
--- 3 Players ---
- **4+ icon removal** — Remove Royal cards marked **4+** before play.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **3-player game** — Game ends after each player has been Sheriff **3 times**.
- **4-5 player game** — Game ends after each player has been Sheriff **2 times**.

#### End-of-Game Procedure
1. **Discard hands** — All cards still in hand are discarded and score **0**.
2. **Reveal stand contraband** — Flip all face-down contraband in each Merchant Stand face up.
3. **Base score** — For each player, total:
   - Coin value in personal stash
   - Printed value of all legal goods in stand
   - Printed value of all contraband/Royal Goods in stand
4. **King/Queen bonuses** — Award legal-goods majority bonuses by good type (apples, cheese, bread, chicken):
   - **King bonus** to highest total quantity for that legal good
   - **Queen bonus** to second-highest total quantity for that legal good
   - Royal Goods count as **2 or 3 copies** of their matching legal good when determining these majorities
5. **Final total** — Add bonuses to base score.

#### Winner and Tiebreakers
- **Winner** — Highest final score wins.
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Most legal goods in stand.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — Most contraband goods in stand.
- **Tiebreaker 3** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First-Rounds Priorities
- **Credibility seed** — Make a few honest legal bags early to increase later bluff equity.
- **Discard tracking** — Watch both discard piles every Market phase to map likely hands.
- **Majority lane choice** — Commit early to at least one legal-good race (often Apples or Cheese).
- **Liquidity buffer** — Keep reserve coins so one failed inspection does not collapse your next round.

#### Early Sheriff Priorities
- **Signal-based inspections** — Inspect based on draw/declaration inconsistencies, not table emotion.
- **Penalty EV awareness** — Over-inspecting truthful bags burns your own economy.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Mistakes
- **Early contraband flood** — Repeated contraband-heavy bags attract inspection and penalty bleed.
- **Unbelievable declarations** — Declaring premium legal stacks after suspicious market draws invites checks.
- **Count mismatch errors** — Declared card quantity must match bag exactly; mistakes create auto-suspicion.
- **Bribe overpayment** — Paying huge guaranteed bribes for low-value cargo is often negative value.
- **Bonus neglect** — Ignoring King/Queen races until late game often loses to legal-good specialists.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Reputation vs Burst
- **Reputation line** — Stay mostly honest early to buy cheaper future pass-throughs.
- **Burst line** — Spike contraband now when Sheriff confidence is low and table reads are weak.

#### Decision 2: Sheriff Strictness Profile
- **Strict line** — Catch more lies but risk paying many truthful penalties.
- **Loose line** — Preserve cash but allow contraband growth across opponents.

#### Decision 3: Scoring Portfolio
- **Legal-majority line** — Push King/Queen bonuses with reliable legal deliveries.
- **Contraband-value line** — Maximize raw card value and coin, accepting inspection volatility.
- **Hybrid line** — Mix legal majority with selective contraband spikes for best median outcomes.

metadata.notes: Reformatted to scan-first style with explicit Step 0 player-count audit and exact per-count rule differences from the provided Sheriff of Nottingham rulebook text.
